questions.question: Who is the main character in the story and what does he like to do?
questions.answer: The main character is the speaker's brother. He is a young boy who loves to build things, experiment with tools, and come up with new ideas for inventions.
answer_outline: Identify the brother as the protagonist

questions.question: What was the 'Great Invention' described in the chapter?
questions.answer: The great invention was a special machine designed to help with daily chores or solve a specific problem (depending on the specific text version, usually something like an 'Automatic Bed-Maker' or a 'Homework Helper').

questions.question: What materials did the brother use to build his invention?
questions.answer: He used everyday items found around the house, such as cardboard boxes, old springs, pieces of string, sticky tape, and some of his father’s old tools.
answer_outline: Highlight the use of simple tools

questions.question: Where did the brother work on his project?
questions.answer: He usually worked in a messy corner of the garage or his bedroom, which he called his 'secret laboratory.'
answer_outline: Mention the name he gave it (laboratory)

questions.question: How did the speaker (the sibling) feel about the brother's work at first?
questions.answer: At first, the speaker was a bit skeptical and thought the brother was just making a big mess with his 'silly' ideas.
answer_outline: Lack of belief in the project

questions.question: What happened the first time the brother tried to start the machine?
questions.answer: The first time he tried it, the machine made a loud 'clink-clank' sound, shook violently, and then fell apart into a pile of junk.

questions.question: Did the brother give up after the first failure?
questions.answer: No, he did not give up. He simply sat down, looked at what went wrong, and started fixing it with a smile on his face.

questions.question: Why did the brother wear a pair of 'scientist goggles'?
questions.answer: He wore them to look like a real inventor and to protect his eyes while he was working with springs and tools.

questions.question: What role did the parents play in the story?
questions.answer: The parents were supportive. Even though they worried about the mess, they encouraged the brother to keep trying and provided the materials he needed.

questions.question: What was the funniest part of the invention's design?
questions.answer: The funniest part was that it had a mechanical arm made of an old umbrella that was supposed to pat you on the back when you finished your work.

questions.question: How did the invention finally work in the end?
questions.answer: After many attempts, the machine finally stayed together and performed its task, even if it was a bit loud and clumsy.

questions.question: What does this story teach us about 'Mistakes'?
questions.answer: The story teaches us that mistakes are just steps toward success. Every time the brother failed, he learned something new to make his invention better.

questions.question: Describe the 'Blueprints' the brother drew.
questions.answer: The blueprints were messy drawings with lots of arrows and circles, showing where every nut and bolt should go.

questions.question: What noise did the machine make when it was working well?
questions.answer: It made a happy 'whirr-whirr-pop' sound that made everyone in the house laugh with joy.

questions.question: How did the speaker's opinion change by the end of the story?
questions.answer: The speaker went from being skeptical to being very proud of their brother. They realized that having big ideas is a wonderful thing.

questions.question: What did the brother do with the invention after it was finished?
questions.answer: He showed it to all his friends and family, and then he started thinking about how to build an even better version.

questions.question: Why is 'Imagination' important in this story?
questions.answer: Imagination is what allowed the brother to see a 'Great Invention' inside a pile of junk and old boxes.

questions.question: Name one tool the brother used from his father's toolbox.
questions.answer: He used a screwdriver to tighten the loose screws on the cardboard frame of his machine.

questions.question: What was the brother's 'Ultimate Goal'?
questions.answer: His ultimate goal was to make life easier for his family by creating things that could do the boring work for them.

questions.question: How did the brother feel when the machine finally 'clicked' into place?
questions.answer: He felt extremely excited and did a little 'victory dance' in the middle of the garage.

questions.question: Was the invention perfect? Why or why not?
questions.answer: No, it wasn't perfect. It was still a bit shaky and made a lot of mess, but it worked well enough to prove his idea was good.

questions.question: What kind of personality does the brother have?
questions.answer: He is curious, hard-working, creative, and very patient because he doesn't get upset when things go wrong.

questions.question: Why did the brother call it a 'Great' invention?
questions.answer: He called it 'Great' because he was proud of the hard work he put into it and because he believed in the power of his own ideas.

questions.question: What would happen if the brother had no tools?
questions.answer: If he had no tools, he would probably use his imagination to find other ways to put things together, like using knots and glue.

questions.question: Summarize the story in two sentences.
questions.answer: The story is about a creative boy who builds a machine from household junk and refuses to give up when it fails. In the end, his hard work pays off, and his family is proud of his 'Great Invention.'
